programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A loan shark, a stockbroker and a group of women pose problems for Dr Sloan as he tries to track down the person responsible for a friend's murder. Dick Van Dyke stars(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: One of Steve's fellow officers is shot during an undercover raid, but Dr Sloan suspects the death was no accident. As the pair investigate the lives of the officer who pulled the trigger and the victim's widow, they are drawn into a world of blackmail and murder. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse and Dr Sloan are infected with a virus and face imminent death unless Steve and Amanda can find a cure. Crime drama, starring Dick and Barry Van Dyke, with Victoria Rowell, Charlie Schlatter and Michael Tucci(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. Under the guidance of therapist Dr Rachel Cranston, his memory begins to return and suspicion falls on his ex-lover EJ Barrett. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Sarah Jane Morris(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a coastguard officer on board an abandoned cargo ship draws the team's attention to a family of Lebanese refugees hoping to find a new home in America. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Two attempts are made on Chegwidden's life, prompting the team to investigate who might want to kill him and why - but they find the culprit closer to home than they had expected(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Harm and Mac go for a ride in a vintage aeroplane, but a malfunction forces them to land in a mountain clearing. As they languish miles away from civilisation, the pair are attacked by a gang of poachers who have killed a game warden. Drama, starring David James Elliott(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The discovery that a murder weapon used in Idaho matches one used in a national security case back in LA brings Sam and Callen into contact with NCIS: Red, a mobile team of agents who live and work together solving crimes across the US - and soon they are signed up as honorary members to help piece together the puzzle. Kim Raver (24) and John Corbett (Sex and the City) guest star(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The LA and Red agents continue their collaborative effort to track down the elusive Robert Spears, with intelligence from captured terrorist Sayed leading them to El Centro in Mexico. Kim Raver (24) and John Corbett (Sex and the City) guest star with Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An innocent drink at a bikini bar takes a turn for the worse when cyanide-laced water claims the lives of an exotic dancer and a navy lieutenant, and leaves a pilot fighting for his life. programme.desc: The body of a Mexican cartel boss is snatched from a morgue in Tijuana, so Kensi and Deeks head to the scene of the crime, while Callen and Sam stay behind to ponder the possibility they have a mole in their ranks. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gary hunts for Joe, desperate to find his fugitive brother before the vengeful gangsters get their hands on him, but dramatic developments make him wonder if he's been manipulated all along. Elsewhere, as Tony comes to terms with the enormity of what he's done, a witness comes forward accusing him of reckless driving, while Jim is moved to declare his true feelings for June(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Andrea fears her true identity as an undercover reporter could be blown when a journalist visits Sun Hill, while Jim decides to follow a psychic's advice and propose to June. Sheelagh is mortified to see her son arrested for a homophobic hate crime and Lance is furious at Adam for revealing he's gay, while Steve and Cameron find the dead body of a young thug - could he be the latest victim of Gabriel's vigilante campaign?(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Consumed by her desire for revenge, Kerry confronts Gabriel and tells him she intends to prove he's the serial rapist. He isn't remotely fazed by her threats, however, and continues to goad Cameron, provoking a violent outburst in the canteen. Rob pursues his vendetta against the Radford clan by making a rash accusation, and Sheelagh finally loses patience with Lance's persistent hints that her son might be gay(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gabriel demands Cameron be expelled from Sun Hill following the canteen brawl and it seems only Kerry's testimony can save him - but it's doubtful she'll be willing to speak out after jeopardising the hunt for the serial rapist. As the pressure mounts on Cameron, he decides to throw in the towel and return to Australia, leading to an emotional confrontation with his ex-fiancee. Meanwhile, Gina fears Jonathan Fox's passion for her is cooling and Neil becomes increasingly attracted to Andrea - but will he realise she's an undercover reporter?(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: As a grief-stricken Vic and Lucy struggle to come to terms with Peggy's death, life goes on at the health centre, where a man turns nasty when his wife refuses to continue her IVF treatment. Meanwhile, Karen joins the team, but isn't exactly welcomed with open arms, while her husband David starts work at the Goddard's. Rural drama, starring Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Tension mounts between Karen and Jacqui, and an elderly patient confides in Chris, while David comes t o Henry's aid at Goddard's - much to Simon's displeasure. Elsewhere, a patient suspected of having cancer tries to persuade his daughter to accept the new love in his life(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are called in to investigate when gardens entered for a village contest are attacked by a disease. Further sinister undercurrents are exposed when reigning champion Donald Westward is found dead and recriminations start to fly as buried rivalries come to the surface. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny returns from her stint at the London Hospital and heads straight into one of the most emotive cases of her career when she and Sister Evangelina assist at the birth of a baby with spina bifida. As the parents struggle to come to terms with their son's life-limiting condition, Jenny, who has never delivered a disabled child, also fails to comprehend the far-reaching implications for the family. Meanwhile, Nonnatus House plays host to an acquaintance of Chummy - the Rev Applebee Thornton, an eccentric cleric who befriends Jane. Jessica Raine, Pam Ferris and Dorothy Atkinson star, with a guest appearance by Jason Watkins (Trollied)(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The locals excitedly prepare for the summer fete, featuring a baby show for which Trixie volunteers to secure a celebrity judge. Not everyone is in the mood for a party, however, as Jenny finds when she meets Nora Harding, a mother-of-eight distraught that she will soon have another mouth to feed. The midwife does her best to reassure the desperate mum, but her words fall on deaf ears and Nora ends up taking matters into her own hands. Jessica Raine and Helen George star, with a guest appearance by Sharon Small (Mistresses)(n)

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the mystery drama. The sleuthing senior citizen is urged by an old friend to visit her sister as she is convinced something is wrong. Miss Marple heads for the country to investigate and it is not long before a heated argument in a locked room ends with a gunshot that leaves a man dead. Jean Simmons and Joss Ackland guest star, with Joan Hickson(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective takes on his most challenging case as he searches for Linda's father-in-law, with the only clue to his whereabouts being an incident on board a submarine 25 years ago. As the case takes a devastating turn, Wallander is plunged into the shady world of Cold War-era politics and faces a battle to protect his daughter. Final episode of the crime drama, starring Kenneth Branagh(n)

When an artist's impression of a skeleton found in an archaeological dig matches one of the youngsters, the team tries to determine the identity of the paedophile. Starring Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston and Claire Goose(n)

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: In the tense build-up to a world trade summit in Edinburgh, Rebus looks forward to a well-earned day off - until Siobhan rings to say a body has been found in the grounds of the hotel hosting the conference. When a government minister is later killed in a fall from the castle ramparts, the abrasive copper comes up against Commander Steelforth of the SO12 unit providing security for the summit and, not for the first time, finds himself at odds with the Establishment. Julie Graham guest stars, with Ken Stott and Claire Price(n)

programme.title: Lorna Doone
programme.desc: Lavish adaptation of RD Blackmore's novel of lovers, outlaws and warring families in 17th-century Exmoor. Amelia Warner stars as the eponymous heroine, with Queer as Folk's Aidan Gillen as her evil aristocratic cousin, who wants her for himself at any cost. The cast also includes Richard Coyle, Martin Clunes, Barbara Flynn and Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: Countryfile

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For generations, governments have sought to simplify the benefits system. The latest attempt, Universal Credit, merged six benefits into one and was supposed to make life better for claimants - many of them vulnerable. But it's been controversial from the start. Last year, Panorama went to Flintshire in North Wales, one of the first areas to see it rolled out, to reveal the system was creating hardship. Now, the government has made some changes, so Reporter Catrin Nye is returning. Was what she saw a year ago just teething problems, or are there fundamental problems with the new benefit?(n)

programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with actor Brian Cox and comedian and actress Roisin Conaty joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Dwayne Johnson and Kevin Hart talk about their new movie Jumanji: The Next Level, Jodie Whittaker discusses the new season of Doctor Who and Michael Palin reflects on his latest travel book, North Korea Journal. Plus, music by - and a word from - singer-songwriter Harry Styles(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: An attempt to breed cheetahs in captivity is made difficult since the male involved is already part of a different mating pair. Plus, a look at the annual fireworks displays(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a semi in Birmingham, a plot of land in Chesterfield, and a grand plan for three flats in Lewisham(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A farmer and his daughter are both rushed to A&E with injuries just as their family is busy with sheep-shearing season, and a crofter has to have a kidney removed(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score an exercise harness, a portable hanging device for valuables, and a flexible hairbrush(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Plant hosts from the Royal Highland Centre, near Edinburgh, as Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper assist two teams competing to make the greater profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara lock horns, and Emma feels sorry for Gareth. Zara helps a teenager understand the reasons behind his behavioural problems, only to enrage his mother(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: An investigation gets under way when a girl is left for dead at a boys' school fete. Mark Williams stars in this drama, based on the stories by GK Chesterton(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an osteopath find a country retreat in Herefordshire, and visits an archaeological dig at Dorstone Hill which is revealing new facts about the Neolithic age(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come to the aid of Manjinder in Rutland, East Midlands, who wants to create an interactive space to inspire her two small nephews(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a family butcher's trusty sausage-making machine, a treasured Edwardian postcard album, and an Iraqi hot water urn, known as a samovar(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney grows excited about her impending hotel stay, while Callum shares his concerns about Leo with Jay. Is Whitney in danger? Martin is wary when Tubbs suggests a way they can make more money. Louise's baby stabilises but remains in intensive care as a guilty Lisa watches on(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is confined to a psychiatric unit, and refuses to accept that she has a problem. However, a visit from Fletch, Sacha and Elliot convinces her that things have to change for Emma's sake. Chloe receives some life-changing news, and decides that she has to tell Evan's sister the truth about her dead brother. Zav and Cameron try to save the life of a girl who has been stabbed, only to discover a disturbing truth behind the attack(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Leo deludes himself that his parents still love each other, and as Julia's wedding draws closer, he hatches a plan to get them back together. He finds himself with an unexpected ally in Benjamin's half-brother - whom Julia never even knew existed. His arrival leaves her suddenly aware how little she knows about her fiancee. Benjamin is forced into a desperate decision that only exposes his deceptions further, while Leo is forced to painfully acknowledge that the happy life he believes his family used to enjoy never really existed(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: An in-depth profile of the five candidates who have made it through to the 2019 final of the business challenge. The programme examines what drives the ambitious would-be entrepreneurs, and looks back at the highs and lows of their time on the series, as they have grappled with the assorted money-making challenges placed before them. Featuring contributions from family and friends, as well as Alan Sugar's faithful aides Karren Brady and Claude Littner, who offer their assessment on how the finalists have fared so far(n)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Documentary following life at Newcastle hair salon Longlox, charting the highs and lows of its stylists. The junior staff members get into trouble, extensionist Saffron gets an intimate tattoo, and owner Sammyjo has questions about the mysterious man in her life(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke head to Northamptonshire to help create a low-allergen garden for a couple whose young son is affected by severe allergies(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don journeys to the Mediterranean, the cradle of European civilisation, for the next stage in his horticultural tour. In Italy, he witnesses the elaborate stone and water gardens created during the high Renaissance. He also investigates the influence of Moorish culture on European gardens, visiting the Aguedal in Marrakesh, and Spain's magnificent Alhambra(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Helen Skelton explore the Tees Valley, while Tom Heap investigates whether enough is being done to protect UK farming and food production from flooding(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last programme of knockout week, the second group of five face two tough tests. First they are given three hours, with limited equipment and facilities, to produce a knockout plate that showcases their versatility and style. This dish is judged by a group of diners specialising in the street food and pop-up industry - and the contestant that wins the most votes sails through to the semi-finals. The other four are left to produce a showstopper dish in just 90 minutes - hoping it will be enough to get them through(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussilon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the final of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 19 frames, with a maximum of eight to be played in this session. Ronnie O'Sullivan dominated the opening session of last year's showpiece match, taking a 6-2 lead over Mark Allen. Presented by Hazel Irvine, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, John Virgo and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the annual event, held this year in Lisbon, Portugal. With commentary by Paula Radcliffe and Steve Cram(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cheese factory in Gateshead, where 3,000 tonnes of spreadable cheese are produced every year. He follows the production of jalapeno chilli flavoured cheese, from a 28,000-litre delivery of milk to 5,400 squeezy tubes. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey finds out how bacteria are responsible for the huge variety in smell, taste and appearance of different types of cheese. She also learns that some smelly cheeses contain the same bacteria that are responsible for stinky feet(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Concluding coverage of the final of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 19 frames, with a maximum of 11 to be played in this session. Although he had only made sporadic appearances all season, Ronnie O'Sullivan showed no sign of rust as he retained the title last year, overcoming a valiant second-session display by Mark Allen to prevail 10-6 and become the most successful player in the competition's history ahead of Steve Davis with seven triumphs. The result also gave 'The Rocket' a 19th Triple Crown title, having also won five world titles and The Masters seven times, to put him one ahead of Stephen Hendry at the top of the all-time list, as he further strengthened his claim to being the greatest player of all time. Presented by Hazel Irvine, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Defiance (2008)
programme.desc: Three Jewish brothers escape into the woods during the 1939 Nazi invasion of Poland, and launch a series of guerrilla attacks on German forces. As word of their actions spreads, other refugees join them to form a larger resistance movement. However, one of the brothers fears their struggle is doomed. Second World War drama, starring Daniel Craig, Liev Schreiber and Jamie Bell(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Hull, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges - a group of knowledgeable property-obsessed 'nosey neighbours' - return to Leicester to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into the houses before deciding which one they like the best. Narrated by Marcus Garvey(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Elliot is in urgent need of life-changing surgery, and Jac is the only person qualified to perform it - but her struggles with anxiety may put his life on the line. Cameron gets a chance to make amends for his past mistakes, but goes about it the wrong way by manipulating a patient's relative. Carole returns from holiday blissfully unaware of what Dom and Lofty are going through(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit 1926 to explore a case of Leeds landlady Lily Waterhouse who died, it was believed, at the hands of her lodger Louie Calvert, who had only been living at the house for a matter of weeks(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team take on the restorations of a toy cannon, a novelty Victorian inkwell in the shape of a hat and a broken statue of St Fiacre(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: On the road, senior vet Rachael Frew heads to a dairy farm to see a cow with a suspected displaced stomach. Recognising it as a life-threatening condition, she decides to operate immediately and tries to move the stomach back into place by hand. However, it proves a tricky process and the farmer is called upon to assist. Meanwhile, specialist small animal surgeon Martin Fitzpatrick operates on young sheepdog Ellie in a bid to save her leg, which has been badly injured by some wire. Narrated by Kevin Murphy(n)

programme.title: Your Questions Answered
programme.desc: The Liberal Democrat leader takes questions from BBC listeners and viewers as part of a series of special programmes simulcast on 5 Live, the BBC News Channel and online(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces dishes that are good to make in advance, including Mary Berry's fresh fruit salad, James Martin's chicken stew, the Hairy Bikers' autumn pudding, Nigel Slater's bean soup, and Nigella Lawson's ice-cream cake(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Chefs Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio share festive recipes, with actress and singer Martine McCutcheon joining them for the first edition as they prepare the ultimate roasted rib of beef and an indulgent sticky toffee pudding(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes weather presenter Carol Kirkwood back to St Helier, Jersey, to reminisce about a holiday in 1973(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gareth Malone becomes the latest celebrity to dig into his family's past in the popular genealogy show. The choirmaster knows he got the singing bug from his parents, who met while acting together in musicals, and now he wants to know how far back this love of the stage goes. He starts out by looking into his mother's side of the family and strikes gold immediately as his investigations take him to the West End. Along the way, he uncovers tales of the heyday of music hall, a theatre impresario in Dublin and even a performance for King George V(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create festive recipes and chat to celebrity guests, with the Rev Kate Bottley helping them make cherry and bourbon-glazed gammon(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Christina Trevanion value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, while Paul Martin visits Laugharne, the home of his literary hero Dylan Thomas(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the couple who missed out on the final after losing the dance-off. Plus, analysis of the weekend's dances and a look ahead to the contest's climax(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Radio 2's Janey Lee Grace and Tim Smith travel around the Cotswolds and the Malverns in search of antiques, assisted by experts James Braxton and David Harper. Janey's vintage Austin Healey takes her to uncover the story of cross-dressing music-hall superstar Vesta Tilley, while sports fan Tim - in a sporty MG - gets to grips with the art of Shin Kicking and the 'Cotswold Olympicks'(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Forrests take on the Choristers for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Heavenly, Woodsound, Bellplay, Softloud(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Case of Sally Challen
programme.desc: In 2010, Surrey mother-of-two Sally Challen bludgeoned her husband Richard to death as he sat eating lunch at the kitchen table, and was found guilty of murder at her trial in 2011. This documentary follows her appeal against the conviction, with her new defence lawyers arguing that she was the victim of Richard's coercive control, a newly understood type of psychological abuse, and that she should be in prison for manslaughter, not murder(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Maps to the Stars (2014)
programme.desc: A disfigured woman is hired as an assistant to a fading Hollywood star, who has become obsessed with her dead mother and is determined to recreate her most famous film role. Meanwhile, a teen idol goes off the rails under the influence of his overbearing parents and a chauffeur harbours dreams of becoming a writer. David Cronenberg's drama, starring Julianne Moore, Mia Wasikowska, John Cusack and Robert Pattinson(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: The leaders of the two main political parties - Boris Johnson of the Conservatives and Jeremy Corbyn of Labour - go head to head in a forum chaired in Southampton by Nick Robinson. Following unprecedented turbulance within Westminster over the past three-and-a-half years, the two will hope to explain why their objectives and visions point out the correct path for Britain to take over the next five years. This marks what will probably be the last occasion the two will meet prior to Polling Day on December 12(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. The added difficulty for the candidates is that they are given no clues as to which of the cities the various items are located in. In Oxford, timewasting leads to a baffling back-and-forth for one team, while in Cambridge, the other team gamble with their negotiations. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Helen Skelton explore the Tees Valley, while Tom Heap investigates whether enough is being done to protect UK farming and food production from flooding(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Return of the programme following life at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, Shetland. A Norwegian sailor is brought in after fracturing his ankle aboard his ship, while new junior doctor Freddie Searight must dust off his French to treat an Indonesian fisherman who comes into A&E with an eye injury(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a well-travelled leather case, a home-made telescope built from scrap metal and a 100-year-old bowl with an extraordinary history(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane, finds out how the townsfolk of Innisfail withstand the tropical storms that strike the region and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari. At Mungalla Cattle Station, he hears how aboriginal people faced brutality at the hands of 19th-century settlers and after a singalong, he makes tracks for his end destination of Townsville - the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from the BBC archives, focusing on classic winter warmers that work equally well in spring, autumn and summer. The menu includes Keith Floyd's beef provencale, a chicken dumpling soup from Rachel Khoo, the Hairy Bikers' apple pie and James Martin working wonders with the humble jacket potato(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by actor Christopher Biggins as they share culinary secrets, perfect for creating delicious festive flavours for Christmas(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes dancer Anton du Beke back to Blackpool to reminisce about a seaside holiday in 1981, including taking him for a tide on a vintage tram(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Anne Reid is keen to find out about her father's family, but only has a couple of clues to go on - the name of a house in Scotland and an old story that his ancestors were ministers in the church. This puts her on the trail of her great-great-grandfather John Reid in Fife, who turns out to have been a teacher and not a minister. However, he also had a less-honest sideline which takes the story in an altogether different direction(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create festive dishes with the help of The One Show's Alex Jones, including a turkey salad, figs stuffed with stilton, and a chocolate and chestnut roulade(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, and pays a visit to Europe's last water-powered spade mill. With experts Mark Stacey and Caroline Hawley(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal talks to the couple who won the last spot in the Strictly final, and previews the songs the couples will be dancing to on Saturday in Tunes on Tuesday(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Hollyoaks stars Will Mellor and Nick Pickard hunt for bargains in north west England, assisted by experts Paul Laidlaw and Steph Connell. Along the way, they uncover the unusual history of Merseyside village Port Sunlight and learn about glass-making, before bringing their finds - a pocket watch and a ship's porthole - to Greenwich to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first round of semi-finals week. The eight remaining contestants face two challenges, to be judged by Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing. Firstly, they must prepare a dish that captures the moment that triggered their own love of food. After this, they move on to the invention test, in which they must make a flawless vegan meal(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef concludes his tour with trips to the Languedoc and Provence. In the border town of Uzes, Rick samples salt cod and marvels at a colourful array of French patisserie - selecting a seasonal fig tart to have with his morning coffee. He moves on to the harbour town of Cassis to try North African lamb chorba cooked by a local fisherman, while back in his country kitchen, he prepares a confit tomato and aubergine tarte tatin(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Desiree Burch hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Paul McCaffrey and Jonny Pelham(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the 14th round of fixtures, including Los Angeles Rams v Seattle Seahawks(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Reading v Arsenal at Adams Park, Chelsea v Manchester City at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Manchester United v Everton at Leigh Sports Village(n)

programme.title: Canals - The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor discovers how carving up the landscape in order to build canals helped further man's understanding of the earth below. William Smith, a surveyor on the Somerset Coal Canal, discovered a way of ordering layers of rocks and eventually created the first geological map of England and Wales. His ideas about the age and formation of the earth were developed long before Darwin published his theories of evolution(n)

programme.title: James Galway at the BBC
programme.desc: The flautist, who turned 80 today, reflects on a career spanning more than 40 years. He looks back on TV appearances from Parkinson and Top of the Pops to Wogan and Songs of Praise(n)

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Two-part programme exploring the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examining the rich culture and troubled past. In the first episode, Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The presenters look back on the major stories of 2019 - including the New Horizons mission to the most distant world mankind has ever visited, and the first picture of a black hole(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: An edition first shown in July 2012 focusing on Curiosity, Nasa's mission to investigate the possibility of life on Mars. The agency hoped to land a nuclear-powered rover vehicle, which cost $2.5billion to develop, on the surface of the planet by winching it down from a rocket-powered crane, then use it to explore the terrain. The programme features contributions by the project's chief scientist John Grotzinger and discovers the lessons experts learned from previous expeditions, many of which ended in failure(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history behind the telescope, from the earliest experiments with optics in eighth-century Baghdad to the Hubble Space Telescope. The programme also reveals how Galileo constructed his own telescope and how a New York property boom led to the discovery of ways of measuring a star's distance from Earth(n)

programme.title: Tales of Winter: The Art of Snow and Ice
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how man's struggle with the onset of winter has been depicted by Western painters across the centuries. Among the works featured is Pieter Bruegel the Elder's Hunters in the Snow from 1565 - one of the first paintings to sum up the season as a vision of beauty. With contributions by Grayson Perry, Will Self and Don McCullin, among others(n)

programme.title: Music Moguls: Masters of Pop
programme.desc: Leading publicist Alan Edwards narrates this instalment looking at the PR plots that have helped shape some of the world's greatest artists. With the aid of rare archive footage, he explains how he and fellow exponents, such as Barbara Charone and Andy Saunders, cultivate new bands, manage crises and attempt to maintain the reputation of their long-standing clients. The programme highlights the campaigns behind superstars including Jimi Hendrix, Taylor Swift and David Bowie(n)

programme.title: James Galway at the BBC
programme.desc: The flautist, who turned 80 today, reflects on a career spanning more than 40 years. He looks back on TV appearances from Parkinson and Top of the Pops to Wogan and Songs of Praise(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities as the academic quiz's festive incarnation returns, with the opening first-round match seeing Brasenose College, Oxford taking on Bristol. Among those facing questions from host Jeremy Paxman are Bargain Hunt expert Kate Bliss and Radio 4's More or Less presenter Tim Harford for Brasenose, while the Bristol team includes McMafia author Misha Glenny and geologist and broadcaster Iain Stewart(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore follows the Habsburgs to their dramatic demise. From his struggles with Napoleon III and Bismarck and the suicide of his son Rudolf, to the assassination of his beautiful wife Sisi, Emperor Franz Josef's empire and his family proved impossible to control - but while the Habsburgs headed for extinction, Vienna blossomed(n)

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Christmas Carol Odyssey
programme.desc: Lucy explores the tradition of festive hymns, revealing that there is much more to the best-loved carols than meets the eye, and how they reveal the history of Christmas itself. In the ancient past, pagan fertility ritual the wassail gave rise to door-to-door singing, and was also an integral part of an older midwinter festival was adopted by Christianity when it came to Britain, and was rebranded as Christmas(n)

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon explore Italy's capital in search of the generations of ordinary Romans who have left their mark on the city's culture and gastronomy. In the first of two programmes, Giorgio leads Andrew to some of his personal favourite districts including Garbatella, Italy's first 'garden suburb', which boasts vibrant market stalls and village architecture, where he introduces him to the simplest Roman food. In turn, Andrew introduces Giorgio to some of the most moving pictures by Caravaggio, in what was once the city's foremost church for poor pilgrims(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Eggs: The Genius of Carl Faberge
programme.desc: Stephen Smith explores the life and work of the renowned jeweller, best known for the eggs he made out of precious metals and gemstones for the last tsars of Russia. The presenter talks to Prince Michael of Kent about Faberge items in the Royal Collection, and to Russian oligarch Viktor Vekselberg, who spent $100million acquiring nine of the trinkets. He also follows in Faberge's footsteps, from the Green Vaults in Dresden to the corridors of the Kremlin museum, to discover how a modest family business was turned into the world's most famous supplier of luxury items(n)

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores the art of the Japanese home, the clean minimalism of which has been exported round the world from modernist architecture to lifestyle stores(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore follows the Habsburgs to their dramatic demise. From his struggles with Napoleon III and Bismarck and the suicide of his son Rudolf, to the assassination of his beautiful wife Sisi, Emperor Franz Josef's empire and his family proved impossible to control - but while the Habsburgs headed for extinction, Vienna blossomed(n)

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Christmas Carol Odyssey
programme.desc: Lucy explores the tradition of festive hymns, revealing that there is much more to the best-loved carols than meets the eye, and how they reveal the history of Christmas itself. In the ancient past, pagan fertility ritual the wassail gave rise to door-to-door singing, and was also an integral part of an older midwinter festival adopted by Christianity when it came to Britain, and was rebranded as Christmas(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Eggs: The Genius of Carl Faberge
programme.desc: Stephen Smith explores the life and work of the renowned jeweller, best known for the eggs he made out of precious metals and gemstones for the last tsars of Russia. The presenter talks to Prince Michael of Kent about Faberge items in the Royal Collection, and to Russian oligarch Viktor Vekselberg, who spent $100million acquiring nine of the trinkets. He also follows in Faberge's footsteps, from the Green Vaults in Dresden to the corridors of the Kremlin museum, to discover how a modest family business was turned into the world's most famous supplier of luxury items(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as Westminster University take on the University of East Anglia. Writer and presenter Danny Wallace and Royal Institution Christmas lecturer Sophie Scott are on the Westminster team, while comedian Arthur Smith and weather presenter Darren Bett are on the opposing side(n)

programme.title: Secret Life of Farm Animals
programme.desc: The documentary follows a litter of piglets from birth as they grow up in the Brecon Beacons. The theory that every piglet always returns to the same teat to suckle is tested, while facts such as pigs love mud to keep cool because they have practically no sweat glands are revealed. The animals' intelligence is demonstrated with a series of puzzles, and it is shown the species can be masters at the art of deception. Plus, a farm that uses llamas to guard its sheep and a pet rabbit with a remarkable identity crisis(n)

programme.title: Wild Weather with Richard Hammond
programme.desc: The host investigates the importance of heat - or the lack of it - when it comes to weather. And it isn't just a load of hot air. Without temperature, clouds, rain, snow, dust storms, thunder or lightning would be unable to form. Along the way he builds his own massive dust storm, discovering how sand from the Sahara bounces its way to the UK, and also drops in on a scientist who creates lightning bolts in his laboratory(n)

programme.title: Rome Unpacked
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli and art critic Andrew Graham-Dixon conclude their exploration of Italy's capital. In the second of two programmes, as well as marvelling at the mosaics in the 12th-century Basilica di San Clemente, Andrew takes Giorgio to an ancient Roman schoolteacher's classroom. Then it's on to a true architectural and civic wonder - the vast Testaccio Slaughterhouse, where workers were once paid in offal. Travelling to the Palazzo Colonna, Andrew shows Giorgio the Beaneater by Carracci, a Baroque painting that makes an everyday subject extraordinary(n)

programme.title: Looking for Rembrandt
programme.desc: The story behind Rembrandt's most iconic work, The Night Watch. The events that surrounded the creation of his masterpiece contributed to a 10-year decline in his output, as he devoted his energies to a series of etchings inspired by his extra-marital affairs, while a struggle to manage his debts led him to declare bankruptcy(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet

At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing

With music by Niall Horan and a performance by ballet dancer Carlos Acosta(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth Is Missing

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For generations, governments have sought to simplify the benefits system. The latest attempt, Universal Credit, merged six benefits into one and was supposed to make life better for claimants - many of them vulnerable. But it's been controversial from the start. Last year, Panorama went to Flintshire in North Wales, one of the first areas to see it rolled out, to reveal the system was creating hardship. Now, the government has made some changes, so Reporter Catrin Nye is returning. Was what she saw a year ago just teething problems, or are there fundamental problems with the new benefit?(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa is determined to tell Louise the truth - but is shocked when she discovers her daughter has given birth to a baby girl - while at the hospital, the new mother quizzes Sharon about her absence during the labour. programme.desc: Newsnight and BBC 5 Live presenter Emma Barnett chairs a special edition of the topical debate show from the city of York, featuring a selected audience of voters under 30, who question leading figures from each of the seven main parties ahead of Thursday's General Election. The topics will range from the cost of living, to job opportunities, education, housing, the climate crisis, Brexit and beyond, as these young adults try to ensure their needs are not forgotten about by the party leaders(n)

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the history of Welsh comedy, beginning by looking at the role of stereotypes in comic portrayals in the company of Rob Brydon, Ruth Jones and Rhod Gilbert. Elis also meets some of the new generation of performers who are breaking through, and hears about their attitudes towards Welsh cliches in comedy(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Billy Murphy discovers that his former cellmate Dinny Molloy has just been released from prison and plans to kill him. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney grows excited about her impending hotel stay, while Callum shares his concerns about Leo with Jay. Is Whitney in danger? Martin is wary when Tubbs suggests a way they can make more money. Louise's baby stabilises but remains in intensive care as a guilty Lisa watches on(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is confined to a psychiatric unit, and refuses to accept that she has a problem. However, a visit from Fletch, Sacha and Elliot convinces her that things have to change for Emma's sake. Chloe receives some life-changing news, and decides that she has to tell Evan's sister the truth about her dead brother. Zav and Cameron try to save the life of a girl who has been stabbed, only to discover a disturbing truth behind the attack(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Leo deludes himself that his parents still love each other, and as Julia's wedding draws closer, he hatches a plan to get them back together. He finds himself with an unexpected ally in Benjamin's half-brother - whom Julia never even knew existed. His arrival leaves her suddenly aware how little she knows about her fiancee. Benjamin is forced into a desperate decision that only exposes his deceptions further, while Leo is forced to painfully acknowledge that the happy life he believes his family used to enjoy never really existed(n)

programme.title: Young Parents Hostel
programme.desc: Documentary following three homeless parents who get a place in a hostel, and with the help of careworkers, try to find a permanent home for themselves and their children. Kate faces taking four buses a day to get her son to school, Callum is being monitored by social services to prove that he is a capable father, and Danielle is struggling with post-natal depression(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: An in-depth profile of the five candidates who have made it through to the 2019 final of the business challenge. The programme examines what drives the ambitious would-be entrepreneurs, and looks back at the highs and lows of their time on the series, as they have grappled with the assorted money-making challenges placed before them. Featuring contributions from family and friends, as well as Alan Sugar's faithful aides Karren Brady and Claude Littner, who offer their assessment on how the finalists have fared so far(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Babita Sharma present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Manchester United at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park. Pep Guardiola's side won both meetings with their fierce rivals last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in November 2018, with David Silva, Sergio Aguero and Ilkay Gundogan scoring the goals. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Liverpool at the Vitality Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Watford v Crystal Palace at Vicarage Road(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by shadow chancellor of the exchequer John McDonnell, SNP Leader and first minister of Scotland Nicola Sturgeon and security minister Brandon Lewis. Plus a news review with Gloria De Piero, Sir John Curtice and Sarah Vine(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Scotland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, with reports from around Scotland(n)

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: The Pointless host discusses how being sent to boarding school helped him develop his singing and his religious beliefs, and reveals an obsession with llamas(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Seeing her daughter cope with cerebral palsy has inspired Bradford mum Nicola that a new life in Perth, Australia is the way forward. Nicki Chapman follows the family's progress(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant guide two teams scouring the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors their progress and also finds out about a weird and wonderful collection of bicycles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is at Beamish open-air museum in Co Durham to find out how Edwardians celebrated Christmas. Plus, music for Advent, with Rick Astley performing When a Child Is Born(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin introduces a London couple to his home town of Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, taking them on a trip to the nearby Grand Union Canal and showcasing the local craft ales(n)

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals discover a plane and, following a swift repair job by the penguins, manage to escape the desolate shores of Madagascar. However, they crash-land on the plains of Africa and find themselves face-to-face with their wild counterparts for the first time. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team are in Wiltshire where John Craven is in a wood that's ravaged with ash dieback, and Anita Rani meets a craftsman who's keeping the ancient art of bee skep making alive. Steve Brown pays a visit to one of the country's top wood engravers, and Adam Henson is in Lincolnshire seeing for himself the impact of the recent devastating floods(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the last results show, which will decide who will be going through to next weekend's live final. The scores awarded by judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood are combined with the viewers' votes, and the two lowest scoring couples must compete in a dance-off to retain their place in the contest. With music by Niall Horan and a performance by ballet dancer Carlos Acosta(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra discovers the horrific truth behind the Gobblers' activities in the North. She must now use all her wits to help free those around her and avoid suffering a terrible fate. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth Is Missing
programme.desc: When 80-year-old Maud's best friend Elizabeth fails to turn up for a shopping trip, she is certain that something terrible has happened. Despite living with dementia, she decides to turn detective and sets about trying to solve the mystery, but with her condition it's not always easy to hold on to the clues. Drama, starring Glenda Jackson(n)

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals discover a plane and, following a swift repair job by the penguins, manage to escape the desolate shores of Madagascar. However, they crash-land on the plains of Africa and find themselves face-to-face with their wild counterparts for the first time. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team are in Wiltshire where John Craven is in a wood that's ravaged with ash dieback, and Anita Rani meets a craftsman who's keeping the ancient art of bee skep making alive. Steve Brown pays a visit to one of the country's top wood engravers, and Adam Henson is in Lincolnshire seeing for himself the impact of the recent devastating floods(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the last results show, which will decide who will be going through to next weekend's live final. The scores awarded by judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood are combined with the viewers' votes, and the two lowest scoring couples must compete in a dance-off to retain their place in the contest. With music by Niall Horan and a performance by ballet dancer Carlos Acosta(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra discovers the horrific truth behind the Gobblers' activities in the North. She must now use all her wits to help free those around her and avoid suffering a terrible fate. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth Is Missing
programme.desc: When 80-year-old Maud's best friend Elizabeth fails to turn up for a shopping trip, she is certain that something terrible has happened. Despite living with dementia, she decides to turn detective and sets about trying to solve the mystery, but with her condition it's not always easy to hold on to the clues. Drama, starring Glenda Jackson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Aston Villa v Leicester City at Villa Park, Newcastle United v Southampton at St James' Park, Norwich City v Sheffield United at Carrow Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Amex Stadium. Plus, all the goals from yesterday's top-flight matches(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Reading v Arsenal at Adams Park, Chelsea v Manchester City at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Manchester United v Everton at Leigh Sports Village(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry, Australia cricketer Mel Jones, netball star Serena Guthrie and former England goalkeeper Paul Robinson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle return to Longleat safari park for a week of festive editions, with two Siberian tiger cubs taking their first steps out in the grounds. Jean Johansson helps the keepers give Sprout the porcupine his very own Christmas tree and Kate prepares Christmas dinner for the gorillas(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team assess properties about to be auctioned in Caerphilly in Wales, Bootle on Merseyside, and Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Return of the programme following life at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, Shetland. A Norwegian sailor is brought in after fracturing his ankle aboard his ship, while new junior doctor Freddie Searight must dust off his French to treat an Indonesian fisherman who comes into A&E with an eye injury(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander presents as members of the public unbox, test and score a bed extender, a portable pet feeding bowl and a back lotion applicator(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Stamford, Lincolnshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp challenges the conkers world champion(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen tries to get Valerie and Ayesha to assist in a cover-up. Bear has a life-changing test, while a patient's daughter gives Al pause for thought(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth uncovers a dark secret when a contestant is murdered at a ballroom dancing competition. Drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in Suffolk with a north London couple who have a budget of £640,000, and also meets a goat farmer who made his own escape from the city to the Stour valley(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with ideas to help Holly, a single mum of two in Redditch who has let her garden turn into a virtual jungle(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a well-travelled leather case, a home-made telescope built from scrap metal and a 100-year-old bowl with an extraordinary history(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By Sinn Féin(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For generations, governments have sought to simplify the benefits system. The latest attempt, Universal Credit, merged six benefits into one and was supposed to make life better for claimants - many of them vulnerable. But it's been controversial from the start. Last year, Panorama went to Flintshire in North Wales, one of the first areas to see it rolled out, to reveal the system was creating hardship. Now, the government has made some changes, so Reporter Catrin Nye is returning. Was what she saw a year ago just teething problems, or are there fundamental problems with the new benefit?(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa is determined to tell Louise the truth - but is shocked when she discovers her daughter has given birth to a baby girl - while at the hospital, the new mother quizzes Sharon about her absence during the labour. Leo's cover is almost blown when his mother arrives in the Square, demanding to know why he is in Walford. A desperate Martin confronts Kush after learning that Arthur is going to see Stacey(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Newsnight and BBC 5 Live presenter Emma Barnett chairs a special edition of the topical debate show from the city of York, featuring a selected audience of voters under 30, who question leading figures from each of the seven main parties ahead of Thursday's General Election. The topics will range from the cost of living, to job opportunities, education, housing, the climate crisis, Brexit and beyond, as these young adults try to ensure their needs are not forgotten about by the party leaders(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Billy Murphy discovers that his former cellmate Dinny Molloy has just been released from prison and plans to kill him. Billy enlists Conor and Jock to help him fake his own death, and persuades the lads to let him stay at their house, with Mairead reluctantly agreeing when she hears the story(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with actor Brian Cox and comedian and actress Roisin Conaty joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Dwayne Johnson and Kevin Hart talk about their new movie Jumanji: The Next Level, Jodie Whittaker discusses the new season of Doctor Who and Michael Palin reflects on his latest travel book, North Korea Journal. Plus, music by - and a word from - singer-songwriter Harry Styles(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: An attempt to breed cheetahs in captivity is made difficult since the male involved is already part of a different mating pair. Plus, a look at the annual fireworks displays(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a semi in Birmingham, a plot of land in Chesterfield, and a grand plan for three flats in Lewisham(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A farmer and his daughter are both rushed to A&E with injuries just as their family is busy with sheep-shearing season, and a crofter has to have a kidney removed(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score an exercise harness, a portable hanging device for valuables, and a flexible hairbrush(n)

programme.desc: Thomas Plant hosts from the Royal Highland Centre, near Edinburgh, as Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper assist two teams competing to make the greater profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara lock horns, and Emma feels sorry for Gareth. Zara helps a teenager understand the reasons behind his behavioural problems, only to enrage his mother(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: An investigation gets under way when a girl is left for dead at a boys' school fete. Mark Williams stars in this drama, based on the stories by GK Chesterton(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an osteopath find a country retreat in Herefordshire, and visits an archaeological dig at Dorstone Hill which is revealing new facts about the Neolithic age(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come to the aid of Manjinder in Rutland, East Midlands, who wants to create an interactive space to inspire her two small nephews(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a family butcher's trusty sausage-making machine, a treasured Edwardian postcard album, and an Iraqi hot water urn, known as a samovar(n)

programme.desc: By the Democratic Unionist Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney grows excited about her impending hotel stay, while Callum shares his concerns about Leo with Jay. Is Whitney in danger? Martin is wary when Tubbs suggests a way they can make more money. Louise's baby stabilises but remains in intensive care as a guilty Lisa watches on(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is confined to a psychiatric unit, and refuses to accept that she has a problem. However, a visit from Fletch, Sacha and Elliot convinces her that things have to change for Emma's sake. Chloe receives some life-changing news, and decides that she has to tell Evan's sister the truth about her dead brother. Zav and Cameron try to save the life of a girl who has been stabbed, only to discover a disturbing truth behind the attack(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: The leaders of the main parties in Northern Ireland are invited to take part in a debate, chaired by Noel Thompson, in front of a studio audience(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Leo deludes himself that his parents still love each other, and as Julia's wedding draws closer, he hatches a plan to get them back together. He finds himself with an unexpected ally in Benjamin's half-brother - whom Julia never even knew existed. His arrival leaves her suddenly aware how little she knows about her fiancee. Benjamin is forced into a desperate decision that only exposes his deceptions further, while Leo is forced to painfully acknowledge that the happy life he believes his family used to enjoy never really existed(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: An in-depth profile of the five candidates who have made it through to the 2019 final of the business challenge. The programme examines what drives the ambitious would-be entrepreneurs, and looks back at the highs and lows of their time on the series, as they have grappled with the assorted money-making challenges placed before them. Featuring contributions from family and friends, as well as Alan Sugar's faithful aides Karren Brady and Claude Littner, who offer their assessment on how the finalists have fared so far(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: How to Spend It Well at Christmas with Phillip Schofield
programme.desc: The presenter introduces a guide to this year's must-have toys, and Leicester City striker Jamie Vardy and his wife Rebekah join five families in trying out the latest board games. Phillip competes against racing driver Billy Monger in a remote control car road test, Stacey Solomon checks out fancy dress costumes for children, and children recommend presents under £15(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a festive episode in which celebrity contestants John Inverdale, Judy Murray, Bernard Cribbins and Scarlett Moffatt pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts an I'm A Celebrity special of the game show. Former campmates Joel Dommett, Dean Gaffney and Rosemary Shrager all try to guess well-known phrases and attempt to win the £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity(n)

programme.title: Not the Robbie Williams Christmas Show

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: They've survived until the end after enduring Bushtucker Trials, camp life and each other. But which of the finalists will you crown this year's King or Queen of the Jungle? Ant and Dec reveal which of the campmates will follow in the footsteps of 2018 winner Harry Redknapp. It's been a rollercoaster ride of a contest, with highlights including the windy skyscraper challenge on day one, and the moment Jacqueline Jossa had to face her fear of spiders in an early challenge. While Caitlyn Jenner has become a show favourite, it remains to be seen who will emerge from the camp victorious(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden: The Inside Story
programme.desc: Ross Kemp examines the April 2015 heist in London's diamond district, when a group of career criminals broke into a safe deposit facility and stole approximately £14million in precious metals, cash and jewels, an event dubbed the biggest burglary of its type in British history. The documentary gains access to the secret surveillance material that put the thieves behind bars and features exclusive interviews with the detectives who cracked the case as well as Kenny Collins, one of the thieves involved(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: This time, the comedian meets an agoraphobic dog who is too scared to go outside, before an unusually quiet chihuahua arrives with a suspected injury to her leg.
